item.text: Executing batch 2 for `T2` and `T3`. I’m reading the current test and editor implementations first so the edits line up with the already-landed `useShotGroups` signature change and any local workspace drift.

I’ve got the current surfaces. `useShotGroups.test.ts` is still fully inference-era, and `TimelineEditor.tsx` still carries `resolveFinalVideoShotIds`, the old hook call, and the dead canvas props. I’m patching those next without touching the later-batch canvas/context-menu work.

item.text: The test file can now be collapsed to pinned-only coverage, so I’m rewriting it rather than line-editing dozens of obsolete inference cases. For `TimelineEditor`, I’m removing only the dead final-video inference wiring and keeping the switch-to-final-video flow intact for the later canvas batch.

The test rewrite is in place. I’m running the full `useShotGroups` test module now, then I’ll do a targeted grep pass on `TimelineEditor` because the canvas prop changes intentionally won’t typecheck cleanly until the next batch updates `TimelineCanvas`.

item.text: Executing batch 3 for `T4` and `T6`. I’m reading the current `TimelineCanvas` and `useFinalVideoAvailable` implementations first, because `TimelineEditor` already moved to the new props and I need to line up the remaining interface changes without pulling in the later context-menu/test batch.

I’ve confirmed the tricky part: `TimelineCanvas` still types its menu state through `ShotGroupContextMenu`, which won’t be simplified until the next batch. I’m keeping this batch scoped by removing the live `isPinned` behavior from `TimelineCanvas` and using a narrow type assertion at the menu handoff, instead of dragging the full context-menu rewrite into batch 3.

The two code changes are in. I’m verifying them with targeted greps and diffs now; I’m not running the canvas test module yet because its expectations are still intentionally stale until the next batch updates `ShotGroupContextMenu` and the canvas tests together.

item.text: Executing batch 4 for `T5`. I’m reading the current `ShotGroupContextMenu` in its post-`TimelineCanvas` state first so I can remove the remaining `isPinned` and dismiss/pin branches without dragging the test cleanup batch forward.

The file is still exactly in the pre-cleanup shape the plan described. I’m patching it now so the menu state no longer tracks pinning, the Pin/Dismiss actions disappear, and the remaining video/image actions are keyed only off mode and final-video presence.

The menu cleanup patch is in. I’m doing a final grep and diff pass now; I’m not running the canvas test file in this batch because those expectations are still being rewritten in `T7`, and the requirement here is to keep this batch scoped to the menu implementation.
